old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No woodworking dependency remains because Q'eswachaka uses hand-braided grass cables tied to stone abutments and no timber structural component.

source_shape.source_locator: Building Material and Tension Forces describe local grass fibers twisted and braided into cables tied to massive rock abutments.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The bridge's directly documented structural system is biodegradable grass rope in tension anchored to stone bases.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Wood is not a component of this evidence-locked bridge tradition, so generic timber craft cannot be a direct prerequisite.

ontology_before: All rope bridges inherited timber construction regardless of their actual structure.
ontology_after: Q'eswachaka follows its grass-cable and stone-abutment architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: rope_bridges no longer directly depends on woodworking_basic.
would_reject_if: Authoritative construction documentation identified a load-bearing timber component in Q'eswachaka.
short_reason: The documented bridge uses grass rope and stone, not timber.
